what_it_does: Records ledger metadata for a design-source JPEG image asset named p-14 from a 15-page presentation, mapping a logical image identifier to a binary blob hash, provenance, author session, and creation timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id, address, channel, path, blob, mime, name, dimensions, source, author_session, created, and empty links
Body line reads 'SOURCE design ground-truth — presentation-15p/p-14.jpg'
standouts: Width and height are recorded as 0 for an image asset
Explicit 'SOURCE design ground-truth' label in the body
Empty links array leaves the entry isolated
conventions: Markdown ledger entry with YAML frontmatter
concerns: Zero width/height suggests missing or unextracted image metadata
No relationships/links to other ledger entries
No alt text, caption, or content description for the image
notes: A thin provenance stub for slide/page image p-14 of a design-source presentation.
questions: What does the blob reference resolve to at runtime?
Does 'p-14' correspond to page 14 of the presentation?
How is 'design ground-truth' distinguished from other source labels?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to record a design ground-truth source image; the file does that as a metadata stub, though the zero dimensions mean it does not fully describe the image asset.
intention_signals: To catalog each design-source image with a stable address, blob hash, author session, and creation time so it can be retrieved, audited, and fused later.
contribution: Provides a stable, addressable record that links the logical image p-14 to its binary blob, source label, author session, and creation timestamp.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger markdown file recording metadata for the design-source JPEG image p-14 of presentation-15p, including a blob hash, author session, creation time, and zero dimensions.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog a single design-source presentation image as ground-truth material with provenance and a stable addressable reference.
